2022-01-24.title: Benedict Admits Being at Meeting About Priest Accused of Abuse
2022-01-24.abstract: A statement by the former pope contradicted a previous statement to a law firm investigating allegations of child sex abuse by priests when he was an archbishop.

2022-01-24.title: A Newly Reported Gathering, and a New Headache for Johnson
2022-01-24.abstract: The news came the same day as Mr. Johnson ordered an inquiry into a lawmaker’s claim of Islamophobia.

2022-01-25.title: Omicron Offers Hope Pandemic Could Stabilize, W.H.O. Official Says.
2022-01-25.abstract: The rapid spread of the variant means far more people will have some immunity, at least for now, which might bode well this year for highly vaccinated countries in Europe.

2022-01-25.title: How Australia’s Leader Lost Control of His Chinese Social Media Account
2022-01-25.abstract: A Chinese electronics company said it had bought the WeChat account legitimately, but some Australian officials said it was hijacked, and urged a boycott.

2022-01-25.title: Why Is North Korea Suddenly Launching So Many Missiles?
2022-01-25.abstract: Experience has shown Kim Jong-un that saber-rattling is the best way to get Washington’s attention, especially when global affairs are already in a precarious state.

2022-01-25.title: For Ukrainian Soldiers, a Nervous Guessing Game on the Front
2022-01-25.abstract: Given Russia’s penchant for misdirection, it’s unclear how a military incursion would start. And that keeps the Ukrainian troops on edge.

2022-01-25.title: An Unknown Civil Servant May Determine Boris Johnson’s Fate
2022-01-25.abstract: Sue Gray, who oversees the British government’s ethics inquiries, will deliver a report on Downing Street parties that could topple the prime minister.

2022-01-26.title: Kurdish Forces Battle ISIS After Prison Attack
2022-01-26.abstract: The attempt to break out thousands of former Islamic State fighters from a prison in Syria has swelled into the biggest confrontation between American-backed forces and ISIS in three years.

2022-01-26.title: Australian Government Buys Copyright to Indigenous Flag
2022-01-26.abstract: The flag had been at the center of a dispute, but the move allows anyone to reproduce its design without seeking permission or paying a fee.

2022-01-26.title: The ISIS Hostages: ‘These Children Should Not Have Been There’
2022-01-26.abstract: About 700 boys have been held for years in a prison in Syria because their parents joined the Islamic State.

2022-01-26.title: Diplomats are meeting in Paris under the Normandy Format. We explain.
2022-01-26.abstract: As part of the frenzied diplomatic effort to head off a conflagration in Ukraine, there has been a virtual summit between President Biden and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia, as well as meetings between the top diplomats in Geneva, Brussels and Vienna.

2022-01-26.title: ‘I Feel Really Hopeless’: In U.K., Millions See Non-Covid Health Care Delayed
2022-01-26.abstract: The free health care system was known for delays, caused in part by whittled-down funding. But Covid made waits for transplants, cancer treatments and other essential care even longer.

2022-01-27.title: North Korea Launches 2 Ballistic Missiles, South Korea Says
2022-01-27.abstract: It was the North’s ​sixth missile test this month, the South Korean military said.

2022-01-27.title: Boris Johnson Rejects Calls to Resign
2022-01-27.abstract: Mr. Johnson, the British prime minister, fended off calls for his resignation over claims that he lied about lockdown-breaking parties and his role in authorizing the evacuation of animals from Afghanistan.

2022-01-27.title: Ukrainian police arrest a suspect in a shooting at an aerospace and rocket factory.
2022-01-27.abstract: The authorities identified the man as a young national guard soldier and said he opened fire on fellow soldiers “for undetermined reasons.” The site has been a focus of weapons proliferation concerns.

2022-01-27.title: Russia’s Military, Once Creaky, Is Modern and Lethal
2022-01-27.abstract: A significantly upgraded military has emerged as a key tool of Vladimir Putin’s foreign policy, as he flexes his might around the globe and, most ominously, on the Ukraine border.

2022-01-27.title: A coronavirus case in Beijing’s Olympic bubble is linked to a German team.
2022-01-27.abstract: A supervisor who tested positive is asymptomatic and is now in a quarantine hotel, the German Olympic Sports Confederation said.

2022-01-28.title: What It Looks Like in Northeastern Syria
2022-01-28.abstract: I arrived in a remote part of Syria this week to report on the siege of a prison by the Islamic State.

2022-01-28.abstract: Vice President Kamala Harris’s trip was viewed as more than just showcasing the U.S. presence at the inauguration of Xiomara Castro, the first female head of state in Honduras.

2022-01-28.title: Sarah Palin, who is unvaccinated, dined outdoors in New York City days after testing positive.
2022-01-28.abstract: The unvaccinated former Alaska governor returned to Elio’s, the Upper East Side restaurant where she had been seen dining indoors on Saturday despite the city’s requirement that indoor guests show proof of vaccination.

2022-01-28.title: Let’s Talk Tennis, and Politics
2022-01-28.abstract: This year’s Australian Open was controversial even before it started. How’s it going now?

2022-01-28.abstract: In what amounts to a much-needed diplomatic reset, the U.S. is orchestrating the response to Russia’s threatening moves and making sure to include his European allies.

2022-01-29.abstract: A deadly mistaken ambush by soldiers seeking rebels in the country’s northeast has renewed attention on a special powers act that residents say perpetuates fear and harassment.

2022-01-29.abstract: The “Freedom Convoy” had driven across Canada to reach the capital. The demonstrations on Saturday were raucous but largely peaceful.

2022-01-29.title: ISIS, Thriving in Unstable Places, Proves It’s Still a Threat
2022-01-29.abstract: The Islamic State may no longer be able to control territory, but it has shown in Syria and Iraq that it can still pull off opportunistic military operations.

2022-01-29.abstract: Residents in Mariupol, a Ukrainian frontline city rocked by shelling in 2015, mourn the dead as they brace for a potential Russian escalation.

2022-01-30.abstract: Flight data indicates that the launch on Sunday was the North’s most powerful since 2017. South Korean analysts expect further escalations from Kim Jong-un.

2022-01-30.title: Living by the Code: In China, Covid-Era Controls May Outlast the Virus
2022-01-30.abstract: The country has instituted a wide range of high-tech controls on society as part of a mostly successful effort to stop the virus. The consequences may endure.

2022-01-30.abstract: The fighting was the most intense urban warfare involving American troops in Iraq and Syria since the fall of the Islamic State.

2022-01-30.title: England extends vaccines to vulnerable children ages 5 to 11.
2022-01-30.abstract: Around half a million children in England will become eligible for a first dose of a coronavirus shot on Monday.

2022-01-30.title: Britain Toughens Stance on Russia, as Russia Presses NATO for Assurances
2022-01-30.abstract: Britain moved to broaden the range of sanctions available if Russia invades Ukraine, as Moscow sent an “urgent demand” to NATO to clarify its stance.

2022-01-31.title: Portugal’s Socialists Win an Outright Majority in Parliamentary Election
2022-01-31.abstract: Because of a strong performance in the snap elections, the governing party will not have to form a coalition in the fractious Parliament.

2022-01-31.title: Leader of Australian Megachurch Steps Down After Charge Over Father’s Sexual Abuse
2022-01-31.abstract: Brian Houston, who was the leader of Hillsong, is accused of concealing past abuse by his father, who later died and was never charged.

2022-01-31.title: ‘Everyone’s Looking for Plastic.’ As Waste Rises, So Does Recycling.
2022-01-31.abstract: Plagued by plastic pollution, Senegal wants to replace pickers at the garbage dump with a formal recycling system that takes advantage of the new market for plastics.

2022-01-31.title: In Kazakh Uprising, Reports of Widespread Abuses by Security Forces
2022-01-31.abstract: Through crowdsourcing, rights groups say they are documenting a campaign of beatings and torture.

2022-01-31.title: Liquor stores prepare for battle as New York revisits to-go drinks from the early pandemic days.
2022-01-31.abstract: ALBANY, N.Y. — Amid the gloom and economic devastation that the pandemic first brought to New York, state officials introduced a crowd-pleasing salve: the temporary legalization of to-go alcoholic drinks.
